items.headline: City releases update on Children and Youth Services Master Plan
items.summary: San José City Council reviewed progress on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including programs like the Safe Summer Initiative Grant and Youth Intervention Services. This plan guides how the city allocates resources for youth services and programs.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan gets status update
items.summary: The City Council heard a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, which outlines improvements to San José's library system and how residents interact with library services.

items.headline: Downtown historic building could get major expansion approval
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This could significantly change the building's size and use in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: Major housing project proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road from commercial to residential use and build an 8-story mixed-use building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The project would significantly reshape that corridor.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled, pushing the next hearing to April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed, meaning any decisions on this development are delayed.

items.headline: Major AI Company Expansion at North 1st Street
items.summary: Figure AI is undertaking a $4.1M office tenant improvement on the second floor of 4000 N 1st Street, signaling continued tech sector growth in San José. This large-scale office buildout reflects ongoing investment in the city's AI and robotics hub.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what infrastructure or development work this contract covers.

items.headline: City approves grant for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and arts education in the community.
